title: Fix gcc skip logic
author: claude-agent
description: install_linux.sh was attempting to install gcc/build-essential even when already present. Added command -v gcc and dpkg -s build-essential guards before the Homebrew prerequisites install_tool call, and a skip guard on the brew install gcc line. Logs a skip message when already satisfied.

title: Fix nmap installation hang
author: claude-agent
description: Installing nmap pulled wireshark-common as a dependency, triggering an interactive debconf prompt ('Should non-superusers capture packets?') that blocked unattended installs indefinitely. Fix: pre-seed debconf with wireshark-common/install-setuid=false before the security tooling apt_batch, add DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive guard for the nmap install step, enforce 120s per-package timeout via a dedicated _nmap_safe_install helper, and pass --no-install-recommends to minimise dependency surface.

title: Split-terminal verbose output UI
author: claude-agent
description: Created lib/split_terminal.sh implementing a dual-pane terminal UI: left side (~65% width) displays clean install progress (existing ok/info/warn output), right side (~35%) shows a Unicode box-drawn panel with live verbose output from apt/cargo/pipx/npm scrolling in real-time via a background tail process. Public API: st_init (detects terminal size, draws box, starts background renderer), st_log (appends to verbose log), st_cleanup (tears down background process, restores terminal). Graceful fallback: if terminal width < 100 cols or non-interactive, ST_ENABLED=false and all functions are no-ops. Integrated into install_linux.sh: sourced after supply_chain_guard.sh, st_init called after show_menu, apt_batch modified to tee verbose output to ST_VERBOSE_LOG when split mode is active, st_cleanup called before print_summary.
